Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Computer-generated dream world: Virtual reality for a 286 processor 52 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Microgpt 1778 upvotes, 301 comments
  3. Show HN: Timber – Ollama for classical ML models, 336x faster than Python 96 upvotes, 10 comments
  4. Decision trees – the unreasonable power of nested decision rules 457 upvotes, 73 comments
  5. Microgpt explained interactively 245 upvotes, 37 comments
  6. C64 Copy Protection 40 upvotes, 3 comments
  7. Everett shuts down Flock camera network after judge rules footage public record 139 upvotes, 21 comments
  8. Flightradar24 for Ships 214 upvotes, 46 comments
  9. Right-sizes LLM models to your system's RAM, CPU, and GPU 87 upvotes, 18 comments
  10. Show HN: Vibe Code your 3D Models 48 upvotes, 12 comments
  11. Running Neural Amp Modeler on embedded hardware 23 upvotes, 4 comments
  12. Next-gen spacecraft are overwhelming communication networks 57 upvotes, 16 comments
  13. Ghostty – Terminal Emulator 693 upvotes, 299 comments
  14. Have your cake and decompress it too 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  15. Tove Jansson's criticized illustrations of The Hobbit (2023) 138 upvotes, 62 comments
  16. Little Free Library 102 upvotes, 46 comments
  17. WebMCP is available for early preview 242 upvotes, 136 comments
  18. You don't have to 68 upvotes, 34 comments
  19. When does MCP make sense vs CLI? 348 upvotes, 225 comments
  20. Evolving descriptive text of mental content from human brain activity 8 upvotes, 1 comments
  21. How to record and retrieve anything you've ever had to look up twice 12 upvotes, 3 comments
  22. Why does C have the best file API 99 upvotes, 61 comments
  23. Ape Coding [fiction] 162 upvotes, 108 comments
  24. Why XML tags are so fundamental to Claude 188 upvotes, 131 comments
  25. Show HN: I built a zero-browser, pure-JS typesetting engine for bit-perfect PDFs 44 upvotes, 24 comments
  26. Frankensqlite a Rust reimplementation of SQLite with concurrent writers 31 upvotes, 25 comments
  27. If AI writes code, should the session be part of the commit? 136 upvotes, 157 comments
  28. What our DNA reveals about the sex life of Neanderthals 38 upvotes, 38 comments
  29. Setting up phones is a nightmare 136 upvotes, 173 comments
  30. Long Range E-Bike (2021) 144 upvotes, 218 comments

Last generated 2:30pm, 2 March 2026. Source here.
